titleStmt.title: Letter from James Brown to Sir Henry Arthur Wynne, 5 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James Brown

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This a letter from solicitor James Brown to Dr. Henry Arthur Wynne (1867-1943), Chief Crown Solictor (1916-1922), explaining how the brother of the deceased (Elizabeth Hogg) sent power of attorney from America. Therefore the application on behalf of his client John Duff is at an end.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3888/3

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
BROAD STREET, MAGHERAFLET 5th July 1916 Dear Sir, Elizabeth Hogg late of Ballymully
County Londonderry deceased I think it my duty to inform you that
a brother of deceased called Andrew Hogg
has sent a Power of Attorney from America
to Mr John Malone Solicitor Cookstown who
has applied for Administration so that my
application on behalf of John Duff for whom
I applied for Grant of Administration may
be treated now as at an end. I wrote
you previously on this matter. Yours faithfully James Brown Dr Wynne Chief Crown Solicitor for Ireland The Castle Dublin.
